- [WA] Use one tolerance policy for equality, zero tests, ordering, and output validation. Weak tests have wide margins; adversarial cases mix huge baselines, tiny slack, and near-zero residuals so the same epsilon both merges distinct values and separates equal ones.
- [WA/TLE] Precision-upgrade-only coverage is treated as complete numeric coverage. Wider floats, exact predicates, or adaptive precision can fix a sign, but they do not define ordering contracts, monotone predicates, convergence guarantees, fallback trigger timing, or the resource cost when every comparison is borderline.
- [WA] Epsilon boundary packs are dismissed as ordinary precision noise. Values that are just inside, exactly on, and just outside the comparison boundary can represent different discrete states, so a tolerance tuned for output acceptance is unsafe as a branch, bucket, or containment rule.
- [WA] Put fuzzy equality into sorting, deduplication, or binary-search predicates. Near-chains such as `a ~= b`, `b ~= c`, and `a < c` break transitivity or monotonicity even when ordinary data has clean separation.
- [WA/RE] Use an epsilon comparator inside an ordered container, priority queue, sweep status, or sort. Standard ordering APIs require a stable strict weak order; approximate equivalence can be non-transitive, so the same keys may be inserted, erased, merged, or iterated differently across input order and implementation details.
- [WA] Rank or prune candidates by floating score when the task needs an exact optimum or lexicographic decision. Random scores are separated; structured near-ties discard the true winner before exact validation can see it.
- [WA] Treat floating binary search as proof of an exact discrete answer. Extra iterations still fail on flat plateaus, discontinuities, or integer thresholds separated by less than the chosen tolerance.
- [WA] Store exact integer quantities in `double` or `long double` because the final formula looks numeric. Counts, sums, products, and prefix values above the exact mantissa range lose low bits that only adversarial equality or ordering checks expose.
- [WA] Compute integer floors, ceils, roots, or logarithmic bounds through floating approximations. Large perfect powers, near multiples, and boundary products push the rounded candidate across the exact threshold.
- [WA] Overflow before comparison even though the final answer would fit. Cross products, squared distances, determinants, dot products, polynomial values, and cost differences can wrap before the sign or minimum is read.
- [WA] Overflow before modular reduction inside a correct-looking modular formula. Products in convolution, matrix powers, Garner reconstruction, or lcm-like updates are reduced only after native arithmetic has already wrapped.
- [WA] Use saturated `INF`, capped lcm, or clipped sums as if capping preserved future comparisons. Two states that both hit the cap can still differ after subtraction, division, relaxation, or tie-breaking.
- [WA/RE] Divide or normalize after an epsilon check only proves a value is "not approximately zero." Nearly parallel lines, near-singular pivots, or almost-zero vector lengths can produce infinities, NaNs, or the wrong branch.
- [WA] Decide by cancellation-prone formulas that are equivalent only over exact arithmetic. Determinant expansions, quadratic roots, line intersections, Heron-like area, variance as `E[x^2] - E[x]^2`, and residual cost differences break on near-tangent or near-equal data.
- [WA] Let a near-zero residual decide a comparator after large terms cancel. The printed numeric error may look tiny, but the sign feeds a discrete branch such as winner selection, orientation, event order, pruning, or tie-breaking, where "almost equal" is not a valid third answer unless the proof defines it.
- [WA] Work in probability space until underflow or overflow changes the model. Long products, tiny normalizers, and exponentiated log scores hide rare decisive events or near-equal log-sum choices.
- [WA] Convert rational or decimal input to scaled integers under an assumed precision cap. Short decimals pass; long precision, mixed denominators, or reduced fractions outside the chosen grid break equality and ordering.
- [WA] Compress exact identities into numeric sketches such as moments, floating signatures, or summed powers. Distinct structured objects can share the retained statistics, and final exact checking is too late if pruning used the sketch.
- [RE] Let numeric degeneracy create invalid domains. Near-singular pivots, zero-length normalizations, or `acos` arguments just outside `[-1, 1]` can produce NaNs and infinities from valid boundary inputs.
- [RE] Build transitions from rounded interval endpoints and assume every interval stays nonempty. Tight overlaps, near-touching constraints, or clipped probability ranges can round endpoints past each other and make the modeled transition invalid.
- [RE] Size arrays by scaled numeric quantities whose exact range is part of the statement. Decimal precision, coordinate span, exponent range, or denominator lcm can overflow the size expression or turn a valid boundary instance into a bad allocation.
- [RE] Cast transformed floating coordinates, roots, or probabilities to integer indices after overflow, underflow, or NaN propagation. The model supplies legal boundary values, but the derived bucket, layer, or event index leaves the valid range.
- [TLE] Iterate until geometric or analytic convergence on instances with tiny spectral gaps or near-flat objectives. Smooth random cases settle quickly, while adversarial slow-mixing Markov chains, relaxation systems, or tangency refinements need far more iterations than the time limit allows.
- [WA/TLE] Trust Newton, secant, fixed-point, coordinate-descent, or repeated-relaxation convergence without the hidden contract. Multiple roots, flat derivatives, oscillation, basin switches, stale block optima, and residual-only stopping can return a stable-looking but wrong state or spend the time limit chasing a tolerance boundary.
- [TLE] Replace exact combinatorics with numeric root finding plus repeated feasibility checks. Each check is cheap on samples, but adversarial thresholds with many nearly feasible candidates force many high-precision refinements across the full search space.
- [WA] Treat a bracketing or fixed-iteration root search as if it certified the combinatorial threshold. The numeric routine may satisfy its own tolerance while the task still needs the exact side of an integer, feasibility, or geometry boundary.
- [TLE] Use interval arithmetic or adaptive precision globally after detecting one unstable comparison. Inputs with many near-cancellations make every branch suspicious, so the supposedly rare safe path becomes the main algorithm.
- [TLE] Normalize fractions, big integers, or decimal expansions after every transition. Recurrences with growing denominators, repeated lcm merges, or high-precision polynomial updates make simplification dominate the intended algorithm.
- [TLE] Switch to arbitrary precision everywhere when native arithmetic may overflow. The answer becomes conceptually safer, but all-near-limit inputs force huge operand counts or bit lengths in the hot loop.
- [TLE/MLE] Store exact rationals, symbolic expressions, or interval endpoints for every state. In recurrences, geometry events, and DP transitions, numerator size, expression depth, or interval sets can grow with the operation chain.
- [TLE/MLE] Split states by numeric uncertainty bands instead of resolving the decisive comparison. Near-tie cascades create many possible orderings, interval branches, or active constraints, so the representation grows with the number of ambiguous events.
- [TLE/MLE] Scale numeric values into a pseudo-polynomial table or dense bitset. Samples have small sums, coordinates, times, or decimal grids; adversarial dense reachable sets or huge spans expose the real resource bound.
- [MLE/RE] Allocate by numeric span or product after unsafe coordinate arithmetic. Buckets over `max - min + 1`, scaled grids, or multiplied limits can overflow size calculations or request memory proportional to the full model range.
- [MLE] Memoize high-precision numeric objects using their full normalized form as the key. Slightly different but equivalent fractions, intervals, or polynomial coefficients defeat sharing and make cache size track precision growth rather than state count.
- [MLE] Retain every refinement candidate produced by adaptive precision. Near-equal roots, active constraints, or event times create many almost-identical versions of the same model object, so memory grows with precision attempts.
- [WA/TLE] Use exact fallback only after a cheap numeric suspicion test. The fallback catches obvious traps, but adversarial data hides the dangerous condition until after pruning or forces nearly every case into the slow path.
